sections.title: SUMMARY
sections.llm_prompt: Write the Summary section of an Investigator's Brochure for {product_name}. Concise overview (300-500 words) covering: physical/chemical/pharmaceutical properties, mechanism of action, preclinical pharmacology and toxicology, PK/ADME, clinical experience to date, safety summary, and implications for clinical investigators. Follow ICH E6(R3) Section 7.

sections.title: INTRODUCTION
sections.llm_prompt: Write the Introduction for an IB of {product_name}. Include: sponsor, indication under investigation, developmental phase, historical context, rationale for development, general approach to safe use.

sections.title: PHYSICAL, CHEMICAL, AND PHARMACEUTICAL PROPERTIES AND FORMULATION
sections.llm_prompt: Describe the physical, chemical, and pharmaceutical properties of {product_name}. Include: chemical name, structural formula if applicable (for cell therapy: cell type, source, modification), biological characteristics (potency, purity, identity), formulation composition, storage conditions, stability, excipients, packaging and presentation. {description}

sections.title: NONCLINICAL STUDIES
sections.llm_prompt: Summarize nonclinical studies for {product_name}. Include subsections: (1) Nonclinical Pharmacology - primary and secondary pharmacodynamics, safety pharmacology; (2) Pharmacokinetics and Product Metabolism in Animals - absorption, distribution, metabolism, excretion, biodistribution for cell/gene therapy; (3) Toxicology - single dose, repeat dose, genotoxicity, carcinogenicity, reproductive tox, tumorigenicity for cell therapy, local tolerance. Include summary tables by study.

sections.title: EFFECTS IN HUMANS
sections.llm_prompt: Describe effects in humans for {product_name} if prior clinical data exist. Include: pharmacokinetics and metabolism in humans, safety and efficacy from prior trials, marketing experience. If first-in-human, state so and reference nonclinical justification for first dose.

sections.title: SUMMARY OF PHARMACOKINETICS
sections.llm_prompt: Write a summary of pharmacokinetics for {product_name}. Cover: absorption, distribution, metabolism, excretion (ADME), protein binding, half-life, dose proportionality, special populations. For cell therapy: persistence, biodistribution, trafficking, expansion kinetics.

sections.title: SUMMARY OF SAFETY AND EFFICACY
sections.llm_prompt: Summarize safety and efficacy data known for {product_name}. Include: most common adverse reactions, serious adverse reactions, special warnings (e.g., CRS, ICANS for cell therapies), dose-response relationships, drug interactions, efficacy signals, biomarkers.

sections.title: MARKETING EXPERIENCE
sections.llm_prompt: If {product_name} has marketing experience, describe countries of approval, indications, formulations available, and any post-marketing safety findings. If not marketed, state 'Not applicable - {product_name} is an investigational agent.'

sections.title: GUIDANCE FOR THE INVESTIGATOR
sections.llm_prompt: Write Guidance for the Investigator section for {product_name}. Include: (1) clinical pharmacology considerations, (2) recommended monitoring (labs, vitals, imaging), (3) management of adverse events with specific focus on dose-limiting toxicities, (4) emergency procedures, (5) overdose management, (6) drug interactions, (7) use in special populations (pediatric, elderly, hepatic/renal impairment, pregnancy), (8) contraindications and precautions.

sections.title: REFERENCES
sections.llm_prompt: Generate a references list for an Investigator's Brochure of {product_name}. Include nonclinical study reports, published literature, regulatory guidances (ICH E6(R3), ICH M3(R2), ICH S6(R1), ICH S9 for oncology, FDA cell therapy guidances).
